pr.title: itlikelike cleanup with inlines, add itemstatetable
pr.author: PsiLupan

summary: Cleaned up src/melee/it/items/itlikelike.c with local inline helpers, typed attribute access, repeated-sequence helper reuse, and an added ItemStateTable; adjusted itLikelikeAttributes in src/melee/it/itCommonItems.h so x0 can be accessed as either Vec3* or S32Vec3*. The bot report records 5 new matches and some data progress, but also 2 one-byte broken matches.

key_files.role: Main cleanup target: added zero_vec and it_803F8468 ItemStateTable, introduced small inline helpers, replaced repeated generated-looking sequences with helper calls, cleaned some casts/accessors/control flow, and changed Likelike attribute access through the new union arms.
evidence_refs: metadata.changed_files: src/melee/it/items/itlikelike.c added 137 deleted 197 hunks 28
diff: added const Vec3 zero_vec and ItemStateTable it_803F8468[]
diff: added swapVelocity and likelikeVelocity helpers
diff: repeated reset sequence replaced by it_802D9DDC(gobj)
diff: attr->x0->y changed to attr->x0.x0_f32->y and attr->x4 threshold changed to attr->x0.x0_s32->x
text_corpus.issue_comment: bot report lists itlikelike .data, .sdata2, it_2725_Logic5_DmgReceived, it_802DAD18, and itLikelike_UnkMotion1_Coll as new matches

key_files.role: Header type update for itLikelikeAttributes: changed field x0 from Vec3* to a union with Vec3* x0_f32 and S32Vec3* x0_s32.
evidence_refs: metadata.changed_files: src/melee/it/itCommonItems.h added 4 deleted 1 hunks 1
diff: typedef struct field x0 changed from Vec3* x0 to union { Vec3* x0_f32; S32Vec3* x0_s32; } x0

what_changed: Added an ItemStateTable named it_803F8468 for Likelike motions with animation, physics, and collision callbacks.
Added const Vec3 zero_vec and used it in it_802DC4BC instead of a separate Vec3 symbol shown in the diff as it_803B86D8.
Introduced inline helper swapVelocity(HSD_GObj*) wrapping itSwapVelocity(Item*) and used it from it_802D9B78 and collision code.
Introduced inline helper likelikeVelocity(...) to express repeated stuck/low-movement velocity reversal logic in itLikelike_UnkMotion1_Coll.
Replaced many attr->x0->y accesses with attr->x0.x0_f32->y and one damage threshold comparison with attr->x0.x0_s32->x after changing the header field to a union.
Replaced repeated multi-call reset/change-state sequences with existing helper it_802D9DDC(gobj) in multiple animation/physics functions.
Replaced duplicated dropped/thrown-style setup blocks with calls to existing helpers such as it_802DA960(gobj), it_802DA104(gobj), and it_802DAA10(gobj).

Changed some generated-looking temporary variables, gotos, casts, direct user_data access, and pointer arithmetic patterns into GET_ITEM, GET_JOBJ, HSD_JObjSetRotationZ, array indexing, ABS conditions, and simpler assignments.
Bot report recorded matched-code improvement of +360 bytes and matched-data improvement of +440 bytes, including 5 new matches, but also two broken matches.

smart_moves.text: Factored repeated Likelike velocity reversal checks into local helpers rather than preserving bulky generated-looking collision logic.
evidence_refs: diff: added static inline void likelikeVelocity(...) and replaced nested ABS/temp/goto block in itLikelike_UnkMotion1_Coll
decomp_standards:natural-loops
decomp_standards:infer-authored-source-style

smart_moves.text: Used a typed union in itLikelikeAttributes to document that the same attribute slot is read both as Vec3 data and as S32Vec3 data.
evidence_refs: diff: itCommonItems.h union x0 with x0_f32 and x0_s32
diff: it_2725_Logic5_DmgReceived uses attr->x0.x0_s32->x while velocity code uses attr->x0.x0_f32->y
decomp_standards:typed-fields-over-pointer-math

smart_moves.text: Collapsed repeated reset/change-state call sequences into an existing helper, reducing duplicated blocks across multiple motion handlers.
evidence_refs: diff: several sequences of it_8027CAD8, it_8026D5CC, it_8027C0A8, it_80275270, Item_80268E5C replaced with it_802D9DDC(gobj)

smart_moves.text: Kept matching tactic PAD_STACK narrow where still present, while removing at least one fake-looking do/while padding construct in it_802DAD18.
evidence_refs: diff: it_802DAD18 removes do { UNUSED unsigned char _[0x20]; } while (...) and uses PAD_STACK(0x20)
decomp_standards:matching-tactics-need-evidence

decomp_lessons.lesson: When one item attribute slot is used with multiple vector scalar types, a small union in the owning attributes struct can be clearer than untyped or misleading field access, if the call sites demonstrate both interpretations.
evidence_refs: diff: itCommonItems.h changes x0 from Vec3* to union of Vec3* x0_f32 and S32Vec3* x0_s32
diff: velocity code uses x0_f32->y; damage threshold uses x0_s32->x

decomp_lessons.lesson: Repeated decompiler-style blocks in motion handlers are good candidates for local helper calls when identical call sequences already exist in the same file.
evidence_refs: diff: repeated reset sequence replaced by it_802D9DDC(gobj) across UnkMotion17_Anim, UnkMotion8_Phys, UnkMotion12_Anim, UnkMotion9_Anim, UnkMotion10_Anim, it_802DBA68, UnkMotion14_Anim, and UnkMotion15_Anim

decomp_lessons.lesson: For collision or movement threshold logic, preserving common source forms such as ABS comparisons and helper extraction can remove temporary variables and gotos while still improving match results.
evidence_refs: diff: itLikelike_UnkMotion1_Coll removes temp variables, goto label, and new_var alias in favor of ABS checks inside likelikeVelocity
text_corpus.issue_comment: bot report lists itLikelike_UnkMotion1_Coll as a new 100.00% match

naming_conventions.name: x0_f32 / x0_s32
naming_conventions.lesson: Suffixes identify the scalar interpretation of the same attribute pointer slot rather than inventing higher-level semantics.
evidence_refs: diff: union { Vec3* x0_f32; S32Vec3* x0_s32; } x0

naming_conventions.name: it_803F8468
naming_conventions.lesson: The new ItemStateTable kept an address-style symbol name rather than a speculative semantic name.
evidence_refs: diff: ItemStateTable it_803F8468[] added
decomp_standards:conservative-naming

naming_conventions.name: zero_vec
naming_conventions.lesson: A simple semantic name was used for an all-zero Vec3 constant added in this translation unit.
evidence_refs: diff: const Vec3 zero_vec = { 0.0f, 0.0f, 0.0f }
diff: it_802DC4BC assigns sp50 = zero_vec

naming_conventions.name: swapVelocity / likelikeVelocity
naming_conventions.lesson: Small helper names describe the immediate local operation but do not rename global address-style functions.
evidence_refs: diff: static inline void swapVelocity(HSD_GObj* gobj)
diff: static inline void likelikeVelocity(HSD_GObj* gobj, Item* ip, itLikelikeAttributes* attrs, f32 multiplier)

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Add or recover a TU-local ItemStateTable to match the itlikelike .data section.
evidence_refs: diff: ItemStateTable it_803F8468[] added
text_corpus.issue_comment: bot report lists main/melee/it/items/itlikelike .data from 0.00% to 100.00%
assembly_or_matching_tactics.caveat: The PR slice does not include symbol ownership evidence beyond the diff and bot report.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Use inline wrappers around item velocity logic to influence call shape and clean repeated code.
evidence_refs: diff: itSwapVelocity remains static inline; swapVelocity added and used
diff: likelikeVelocity added and used in itLikelike_UnkMotion1_Coll
assembly_or_matching_tactics.caveat: No author explanation or objdiff detail is included beyond the bot match report.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Replace pointer expression *(it_804D6D40 + 4) with indexed access it_804D6D40[2].
evidence_refs: diff: HSD_Randf() comparison changed to it_804D6D40[2]
assembly_or_matching_tactics.caveat: The slice does not show the declaration of it_804D6D40.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Use GET_ITEM and GET_JOBJ helper/accessor forms instead of direct HSD_GObjGetUserData or saved JObj temporary in selected locations.
evidence_refs: diff: itLikelike_UnkMotion0_Phys changes HSD_GObjGetUserData to GET_ITEM
diff: it_802DAD18 changes HSD_JObjSetRotationZ(temp_r31, M_PI) to HSD_JObjSetRotationZ(GET_JOBJ(gobj), M_PI)
assembly_or_matching_tactics.caveat: The bot report records it_802DAD18 as a new 100.00% match, but the PR contains no review discussion explaining the accessor choice.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Narrow PAD_STACK use remained in some functions after removing more artificial padding or temporary variables.
evidence_refs: diff: it_802DAD18 uses PAD_STACK(0x20)
diff: itLikelike_UnkMotion0_Anim removes PAD_STACK(8)
diff: itLikelike_UnkMotion6_Phys removes PAD_STACK(0x1C)
assembly_or_matching_tactics.caveat: PAD_STACK retention/removal is only visible in the diff; no separate verifier explanation is supplied.

review_feedback.author: decomp-dev[bot]

evidence_refs: text_corpus.issue_comment

review_feedback.author: decomp-dev[bot]

evidence_refs: text_corpus.issue_comment

follow_up_queries: Find later PRs touching itLikelikeAttributes x0_f32 x0_s32 to see whether the union naming was retained or refined.
Search itlikelike.c for remaining PAD_STACK and comments like 'This line is to get 100%' after PR 2212.
Search for follow-up fixes to it_2725_Logic5_Dropped and itLikelike_UnkMotion6_Phys after the bot reported one-byte broken matches.
Search for ItemStateTable it_803F8468 references and whether an item state table naming convention was later established.
Search for other item PRs replacing repeated reset sequences with helper functions like it_802D9DDC.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Use typed union arms when one recovered attribute slot has multiple supported interpretations
accepted_candidate_records.text: PR 2212 changed itLikelikeAttributes.x0 from a single Vec3* to a union with x0_f32 and x0_s32, then used x0_f32->y for velocity and x0_s32->x for a damage threshold. This is a graph-safe example of replacing ambiguous field access with typed union arms when call sites support both interpretations.
evidence_refs: diff: itCommonItems.h union change
diff: itlikelike.c x0_f32 and x0_s32 call sites

accepted_candidate_records.title: Extract repeated item motion reset sequences into existing local helpers
accepted_candidate_records.text: PR 2212 replaced repeated blocks of item reset/change-state calls in multiple Likelike motion handlers with it_802D9DDC(gobj), making the source smaller and more reviewable.
evidence_refs: diff: repeated call sequences replaced by it_802D9DDC(gobj)

source_update_candidates.title: itlikelike.c contains Likelike item state table and motion handlers
source_update_candidates.text: PR 2212 added ItemStateTable it_803F8468 in src/melee/it/items/itlikelike.c with entries for itLikelike_UnkMotion0 through itLikelike_UnkMotion21 callbacks.
evidence_refs: diff: ItemStateTable it_803F8468[] added
source_update_candidates.reason: May be useful as a path fact, but source ownership and current naming should be checked against current repository before promotion.

source_update_candidates.title: itLikelikeAttributes.x0 was represented as a Vec3/S32Vec3 pointer union in PR 2212
source_update_candidates.text: PR 2212 changed src/melee/it/itCommonItems.h so itLikelikeAttributes.x0 is a union containing Vec3* x0_f32 and S32Vec3* x0_s32.
evidence_refs: diff: itCommonItems.h union x0 change
source_update_candidates.reason: Candidate path/type fact only; curator should verify current header state before adding to source corpus.

source_update_candidates.title: Do not treat helper cleanup PRs as clean matches when bot reports broken matches
source_update_candidates.text: PR 2212 improved several itlikelike matches but the bot report also listed two broken matches. Intake records should preserve both outcomes rather than summarizing as a no-regression cleanup.
evidence_refs: text_corpus.issue_comment: 5 new matches and 2 broken matches
source_update_candidates.reason: Could reinforce existing verification-and-regression-ledger guidance; curator should decide whether this warrants a standard update or is already covered.

rejection_notes: Do not promote PAD_STACK(0x20) in it_802DAD18 as a preferred tactic; the PR shows it remained in one place but does not include enough verifier explanation to generalize.
Do not claim the item state table name it_803F8468 is semantically final; it is address-style and may simply be conservative naming.
Do not claim this PR had no regressions; the bot report explicitly lists two broken matches.
Do not infer reviewer intent or acceptance rationale; there were no human reviews or review comments in the loaded slice.
